notes: Vimana state vector has four R3 components: r_cm, omega_gyro, mu_constrained, L_mech.
For torque mu x B, torque is perpendicular to the magnetic moment direction.
Constant torque history integrates to omega(t)=omega0+(tau/I)t.
Normal form dot Lambda=-k(Lambda-1) relaxes to Lambda=1 for k>0.
Magnetic and rotational energy densities match when B=sqrt(mu0 rho) Omega r.
If mu=k M R^2 Omega then mu/(M R^2 Omega)=k.

Ideal topological-protection obligation: constant helicity has zero time derivative.
